title: Speedboat Rental in Estepona: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
For a thrilling day on the Costa del Sol, renting a speedboat in Estepona lets you explore the Mediterranean from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, with half-day rates from €250. BoatHire24 connects you with trusted local owners offering a variety of motorboats, from zippy runabouts to family deck boats.

Where to set off in Estepona

The main departure point is Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, a modern marina with berths for 425 boats and full facilities including fuel and parking. From there you can head west to the sheltered bay of Bahía de Estepona or east towards the marina at Puerto Banús. Many charters also offer pickup from the nearby beaches such as Playa de la Rada or Playa del Cristo.

Further out, a run to the north African coast or the Rock of Gibraltar is possible in a fast boat, though most half-day trips focus on the coast between Estepona and Marbella. The calm waters of the Mediterranean here are ideal for speedboat hire.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboats (Olympic 490), best for small groups of 3-4, from €300 for a half-day. You can book the Olympic 490 for hire directly from its owner.
  2. Bowrider runabouts, perfect for families of 6-7, with shallow drafts for beach hopping, from €400 half-day.
  3. Deck boats, spacious for 8-10 adults, with a sun pad and shade, from €550 half-day.
  4. Inflatable RIBs, agile and ideal for watersports towing for up to 5 guests, from €220 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona?

For a self-drive speedboat, half-day rates start around €250 for a 4-metre boat and rise to €600 for a 7-metre craft. Full-day bookings typically run €450 to €1,000. Private, crewed experiences cost more: a 10-metre yacht with skipper splits to about €800 for a half-day. Use BoaHire24 to browse boats for your dates and compare prices across owners.

Crewed charter rates often include fuel and a skipper, whereas bareboat rentals may add fuel costs separately. Always check the listing for included extras.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Estepona?

Yes, you typically need a Spanish recreational boating licence to drive a speedboat over 15 hp without a skipper. However, many owners on BoatHire24 offer boats with a qualified skipper for around €100 extra, allowing you to enjoy the trip without a licence. The platform's booking FAQ includes details on skipper availability, and the operator confirms exact requirements before you book.

A local tip: in July and August the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, so plan morning trips for the flattest sea and reserve your speedboat well ahead.

At a glance

  • Price band: €220, €1,000 per half-day to full-day
  • Peak months: June, July, August, and September
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half-day) or 8 hours (full-day)
  • Typical capacity: 4 to 10 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Practical information about booking a speedboat

Bookings through BoatHire24 are handled entirely online, with instant confirmation and secure payment.

For full details on cancellation, security deposits, and what to expect, refer to the booking FAQ.

Questions about boat rental in Estepona

Can I rent a speedboat without a licence in Estepona?

Yes, many boats come with a skipper included or as an add-on, so you can enjoy the day without a licence.

What is the best time of year to rent a speedboat in Estepona?

The Mediterranean climate makes May to October ideal, with high season in July and August offering the warmest water.

How far can I go in a half-day speedboat hire from Estepona?

A half-day trip typically covers 15 to 25 nautical miles, enough to reach Marbella or even view the coast of Morocco on a clear day.

What is included in the rental price?

Most listings include the boat, safety equipment, and fuel for a set number of hours; extras like water toys and insurance options are listed separately.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Estepona or list your own. To get on the water in 2026, check availability for your ideal date. If you own a boat in the region, consider listing it to reach thousands of renters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
